rationale: All six AC items are implemented: ResizeObserver-driven sizing, node click navigation (markdown vs artifact), URL-persisted viewport restored on Back, filter chips with edge filtering, staleness-based node colors, View document list link, and updated graph.spec.ts coverage.

findings.summary: Staleness is only ever 'fresh' or 'stale' — the data loader never assigns 'missing', so the red color path in nodeColorClass is unreachable in practice. D3Graph supports it, but +page.ts derives staleness solely from docStale presence.

Non-markdown node click navigates to /artifacts/doc:<id>, which assumes the artifact ID convention 'doc:<docId>'. If the artifacts route uses a different ID scheme this will 404; worth a quick verification.

Viewport persistence uses history.replaceState (not goto) on every zoom/pan-end, which keeps Back semantics correct but bypasses SvelteKit's navigation; acceptable here since no reactive data depends on zoom/pan params after initial load.
